differential_diagnoses: Fever of unknown origin (FUO) due to occult infection
Thyrotoxicosis (e.g., painless thyroiditis or Graves' disease)
next_steps: Obtain TSH, free T4, and total T3 levels
Perform dedicated neck ultrasound with Doppler flow evaluation of the thyroid
Consider radioactive iodine uptake (RAIU) scan if thyrotoxicosis is confirmed to differentiate thyroiditis from hyperthyroidism
Order inflammatory markers (CRP and ESR) if not already trended, and repeat CBC with differential
Consider infectious workup for FUO including repeat blood cultures (if persistent fever), Quantiferon-TB Gold, and coccidioidomycosis serologies
Consult endocrinology for evaluation of suspected thyroid pathology

differential_diagnoses: Thyrotoxicosis (e.g., painless thyroiditis or Graves' disease)
Fever of unknown origin due to occult infection (e.g., endocarditis, disseminated coccidioidomycosis)
next_steps: Measure serum TSH, free T4, and total T3 levels
Obtain thyroid ultrasound with Doppler flow evaluation
Perform radioactive iodine uptake (RAIU) scan if thyrotoxicosis is confirmed
Order blood cultures (repeat if clinically necessary)
Consult endocrinology for management of thyroid dysfunction and potential biopsy if nodule is suspicious
